extraction.one_line_summary: CAMDEN Lennard attacked 40-rider breakaway on final climb ridgeline, time-trialed solo 20km to finish despite Ineos chasing.
tactical_patterns: Ineos aggressively chased initial 12-rider break fearing GC threats, allowing 40-rider counter-group to form without their numbers.
CAMDEN attacked on ridgeline false-summit section after climb's KOM, not the steep gradient, exploiting group's hesitation.
VERONA multiple attacks on descent/ridgeline showed targeting transitions between terrain types rather than sustained climbing.
Large 40-rider breaks fragment inefficiently with riders skipping pulls; solo attackers time-trial better than disorganized chase groups.

rider_signals.CAMDEN Lennard: positive: replicated stage 16 Dauphiné 2020 tactic of attacking Carapaz late climb then time-trialing away
rider_signals.CAVAÑIA Remy: negative: 30km solo effort collapsed on final climb despite strong time-trialing on flatter sections
rider_signals.VERONA Carlos: positive: repeatedly attacked transitions and descents showing superior positioning instinct on technical terrain
rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: Ineos burned Rowe/Dennis/Carapaz controlling 40-rider break threat but retained GC position unchanged
rider_signals.KNOX James: positive: placed in large break as Ineos insurance policy to sit on dangerous moves
rider_signals.MAHARAJH Justin: neutral: gained break via super-tuck descent attack on first major climb showing descending aggression
